surfaces.finding: No id/eventId/journal/Last-Event-ID handling; disconnect or commit-before-fanout loses the notification.

surfaces.cursor: browser transport only; server emits no id
surfaces.replay: automatic reconnect cannot replay without server IDs/journal
surfaces.finding: JSON callbacks have no event identity, resource revision, subscription locator, gap detection, or resync path.

surfaces.consumer: Electron renderer and generic HTTP broadcast
surfaces.durability: filesystem remains authority; watcher event is a hint
surfaces.finding: Forwarding precedes no durable event row and carries no source generation/fileWriterEpoch.
Some stale-run guards exist, but no eventId dedupe, opaque epoch cursor, revision vector, or snapshot barrier.
Rejects payload conflict and records committed location, but uses unversioned stable hash and retries pending without an ADR-34 per-effect evidence class.

surfaces.cursor: provider pre/post prompt cursors, not application event cursor
surfaces.finding: Rich acceptanceUnknown/evidence exists, but payloadHash is not a versioned normalized-intent HMAC and provider cursors cannot be used as the hosted event barrier.

surfaces.replay: completed duplicate resolves via status; unknown timeout blocks retry
surfaces.finding: Correctly refuses blind retry after unknown timeout, but requestHash includes raw body and lacks descriptor/schema/fingerprint/key versions and stable actor scope.

surfaces.durability: sink-dependent and invoked after provider action
surfaces.finding: Action completes before eventSink.record; crash between them demonstrates why hosted state/outbox must be durable before live fanout.

requiredTargetContract.snapshot: same-transaction cursor or retained lower C0 plus revision vector
requiredTargetContract.delivery: listener-before-query durable journal replay with heartbeat/high-watermark requery
requiredTargetContract.reducer: eventId dedupe plus aggregate generation/revision fencing; gaps refetch
requiredTargetContract.externalFiles: watch-before-scan, source hash/generation, observation sequence, fileWriterEpoch

conclusion: Current generic HTTP/team-change flow is a lossy notification path and cannot satisfy ADR-33. Existing provider journals are useful salvage evidence, not a hosted event cursor.
